subject: Re: FW: From Howard Gardner
body:
sorry I missed you.... I met with Richard Branson, who
is working on creating what he terms an elder
statesman council of the world... and asked who I
thought might be appropriate... I told him i have no
idea but he should contact you and inquire , about
your work on trustees.

sorry I missed you.... I met with Richard Branson, who

--- Lesley Groff lesley@nysgllc.com wrote:

-----Original Message-----
From: hgasst [mailto:hgasst@pz.harvard.edu]
Sent: Monday, January 09, 2006 11:52 AM
To: lesley@nysgllc.com
Subject: From Howard Gardner

January 9, 2006

To Jeffrey Epstein
From Howard Gardner

E mail

Hi Jeffrey, and happy new year. As you may have
heard, the Harvard
contingent was well represented at the Renaissance
Weekend-Alan D., Steve K,
and I spent some quality time together-hardly ever
possible in Cambridge!.
The Clintons were there as well, doing their thing.
I learned about an
interesting new book THE UNTIED STATES OF AMERICA,
by Juan Enriques. I
ordered the book and will let you know if it lives
up to its expectations.

The big news in the Gardner household is that we are
now grandparents for
real. Oscar Bernard Gardner was born on 23
December: all are well and all
is well.

I've been in New York with some regularity but
missed you the last few
trips. Hope that when you are next in Cambridge,
with a little free time, we
can get together. By the time you read this, I will
be heading to Latin
America-Chile and Peru-with my son Andrew.

With all good wishes.

Howard Gardner
Hobbs Professor of Education and Cognition Harvard
Graduate School of
Education
14 Appian Way
Larsen Hall 201
Cambridge, MA 02138

Voice: 617-496-4929
Fax: 617-496-4855
www.howardgardner.com
e-mail: howard@pz.harvard.edu

NOTE REGARDING SPAM FILTER: If you reply to this
communication, please be
sure to include the name "Howard Gardner" in the
text of your response.
Otherwise, the spam filter may delete your message.

body:
sounds good

--- Lesley Groff <lesley@nysgllc.com> wrote:

>  
> 
> -----Original Message-----
> From: hgasst [mailto:hgasst@pz.harvard.edu] 
> Sent: Wednesday, November 23, 2005 10:58 AM
> To: lesley@nysgllc.com
> Subject: From Howard Gardner
> 
> November 23, 2005
> 
> To Jeffrey Epstein
> From Howard Gardner
> 
> Hi, Jeffrey. Glad we had a chance to visit a few
> weeks ago.  Since then I
> went to visit three cities in Italy.  I was at a
> meeting with Mort
> Zuckerman, in the Vatican of all places.  Though
> we'd met before, this was
> the first time I had the chance to hear him talk
> about the media at length
> (he gave a paper) and also to talk with him about
> what is happening to
> journalism.  I thoroughly enjoyed and learned from
> our conversation and I'm
> glad that you encouraged me to approach him. 
> Interestingly, we did not
> gossip about Cambridge-probably, just as well.  Nor
> did we decide to launch
> a new magazine!
> 
> Saw on the Edge website that your computer Prize was
> awarded. I trust that
> you were pleased with the choice.
> 
> I've a busy few months ahead of me-minor surgery, a
> trip to Latin America,
> and the birth of my first grandchild. Hope that all
> is well and that I'll
> see you soon.
> 
> Have a good thanksgiving.
> 
> 
> Howard Gardner

body_html:
sounds good

--- Lesley Groff lesley@nysgllc.com wrote:

-----Original Message-----
From: hgasst [mailto:hgasst@pz.harvard.edu]
Sent: Wednesday, November 23, 2005 10:58 AM
To: lesley@nysgllc.com
Subject: From Howard Gardner

November 23, 2005

To Jeffrey Epstein
From Howard Gardner

Hi, Jeffrey. Glad we had a chance to visit a few
weeks ago. Since then I
went to visit three cities in Italy. I was at a
meeting with Mort
Zuckerman, in the Vatican of all places. Though
we'd met before, this was
the first time I had the chance to hear him talk
about the media at length
(he gave a paper) and also to talk with him about
what is happening to
journalism. I thoroughly enjoyed and learned from
our conversation and I'm
glad that you encouraged me to approach him.
Interestingly, we did not
gossip about Cambridge-probably, just as well. Nor
did we decide to launch
a new magazine!

Saw on the Edge website that your computer Prize was
awarded. I trust that
you were pleased with the choice.

I've a busy few months ahead of me-minor surgery, a
trip to Latin America,
and the birth of my first grandchild. Hope that all
is well and that I'll
see you soon.

Have a good thanksgiving.

Howard Gardner
Hobbs Professor of Education and Cognition Harvard
Graduate School of
Education
14 Appian Way
Larsen Hall 201
Cambridge, MA 02138

Voice: 617-496-4929
Fax: 617-496-4855
www.howardgardner.com
e-mail: howard@pz.harvard.edu

NOTE REGARDING SPAM FILTER: If you reply to this
communication, please be
sure to include the name "Howard Gardner" in the
text of your response.
Otherwise, the spam filter may delete your message.

http://mail.yahoo.com
